brief: Create a 24-hour response plan for suspected token compromise in a B2B API platform.

deliverable.audience: CISO, CEO, and incident response lead

facts: Customer reported unexpected API calls from unfamiliar ASN using a valid token.
Logs show 19 tokens used from new geographies in 36 hours.
Platform supports revocation, forced rotation, IP allowlisting, and scoped tokens; 41 percent use scoped tokens.
Audit logs are retained for 30 days and exportable per customer.
Six security engineers and one communications lead are available today.
Largest enterprise customer requires notice within 24 hours for confirmed incidents.
Revoking all tokens would interrupt production integrations for most customers.
constraints: Do not declare breach without evidence.
Include comms thresholds and evidence collection.
conflicting_signals: Broad revocation reduces risk but creates outages.
Waiting preserves continuity but may allow misuse.

grounding_rule: Use only this context pack. Do not invent facts. Label uncertainty when context is incomplete.
